1. Public meaning of “phoxel”
“Phoxel” does not show up as a standard optics / camera / imaging term in the material I found. The main public usage is MIT’s unrelated “Phoxel-Space,” which is about physically exploring voxel data. So for Aurexis, phoxel has to be treated as a project-native technical term, not a borrowed scientific term with an accepted outside definition. Tangible Media Group+1


2. What is physically real underneath it
Light is electromagnetic radiation described by frequency and wavelength, and photon energy follows  $E=h\nu=hc/\lambda$ . So your phrase “measurable photonic waveform signature” is physically grounded: there really are measurable spectral/frequency/wavelength properties underneath the observation. NIST+1


3. What a camera actually gives us
A camera sensor pixel is a physically existing light-sensitive photosite. During exposure, photons hit the pixel area; a wavelength-dependent fraction is converted into electrons; that charge becomes voltage, is amplified, and is digitized into the image value. That means the machine interface is not “light directly,” but light filtered through optics, sensor physics, exposure, and readout. EMVA+2EMVA+2


4. Why pixel ≠ complete truth
In raw color capture, each pixel usually records only one filtered band, not full RGB. Full color is reconstructed later from neighboring samples. So a phoxel cannot be defined lazily as “one camera pixel equals full physical reality.” At minimum, it is a local, filtered, conditioned measurement of a photonic event/field. Basler+2baslerweb.com+2


5. Why spatial relations are not free
Camera imaging is a projection problem: 3D world points are projected into 2D image coordinates through the camera model and its intrinsics. So if Aurexis Core is about physical spatial relations, those relations cannot be treated as raw screen adjacency alone; they need geometry, viewpoint, and calibration in the chain. OpenCV Documentation+1


So here is the tightened working definition I recommend for now:


A phoxel is the atomic Aurexis observation object: a physically grounded, spatially localized light-measurement unit that binds photonic signature and sampled image position into one machine-usable entity. It is not “just a pixel,” and it is not “just a photon.” It is the bridge object between physical light behavior and computational observation. This follows from your definition plus the way photons, photosites, and camera geometry actually work. Hamamatsu Photonics+3NIST+3EMVA+3

the underlying physical light/spatial reality exists before camera observation

the computational phoxel instance is what exists when that reality is sampled into a machine-usable unit


So if we keep your intended vision intact, the camera should be treated as a revealer/instantiator of phoxels for computation, not the creator of physical reality itself. That is the least sloppy version of what you’re aiming at. EMVA+2OpenCV Documentation+2

persistence/change must be lawful

The phoxel’s current lawful relations to nearby phoxels/regions/structures.


This is where Core really starts to become Core.

The phoxel’s trust state.


Should include things like:


confidence

ambiguity

support strength

observation quality

whether it is direct / estimated / promoted

These are the native relation families a phoxel can participate in.


Tier 1 native


adjacent_to

direction_to

distance_to

inside

contains

on_boundary_of

same_region_as

ordered_with


Tier 2 native


overlaps

part_of

same_structure_as

continuous_with

transitions_into

scale_related_to


Tier 3 native later


occludes / occluded_by

corresponds_across_projection

persists_across_time

viewpoint_variant_of


7. Phoxel logic behavior


This is the important part.


Behavior 1 — instantiate


A phoxel is instantiated when Core has enough observed light information to form a lawful atomic observation object.


Not when Core “understands” something.
Just when it has enough to say:
there is a real observation unit here.


Behavior 2 — bind


A phoxel binds image-side observation to world-side possibility.


It does not require full world certainty to exist.
It just requires that the world side be represented honestly as:


unknown

estimated

resolved


Behavior 3 — relate


A phoxel is not meant to live alone.
Its meaning grows by relation.


Core should assume:
single phoxels matter, but phoxel relations matter more.


So a phoxel’s primary logic behavior is to participate in:


local neighborhoods

boundaries

regions

ordered structures

scale hierarchies


Behavior 4 — cluster


Phoxels can cluster into larger structures.


Examples:


boundary chains

regions

contours

grids

repeated patterns

ordered visual sequences


But clustering does not automatically mean semantic truth.


Behavior 5 — persist or dissolve


A phoxel can:


stay stable

weaken

split

merge

dissolve into noise

strengthen into validated structure


This is important because Core must support current-tech vision honestly instead of pretending every observation is stable.


Behavior 6 — promote


A phoxel or phoxel structure can move through 3 levels:


observed


Directly seen


estimated


Inferred but not yet confirmed


validated


Strong enough to use as real structure


Only after this should higher executable behavior be allowed.


Behavior 7 — degrade gracefully


A phoxel must still function under:


blur

compression

low light

angle distortion

noise

partial obstruction

weak signal


This is non-negotiable because Core must work on current mobile technology.


Behavior 8 — remain evidence-bounded


A phoxel cannot claim more than the evidence supports.


A phoxel may say:


“something is here”

“this is likely connected”

“this probably continues”

“world placement is estimated”


A phoxel may not say:


“this is definitely the full world truth”

“this hidden part is certainly present”

“this is automatically code”


8. Phoxel state logic


A phoxel should always carry one of these states:


observed

estimated

validated

unknown (for incomplete world-side aspects)


That makes Core honest by design.


9. Phoxel behavior toward execution


A phoxel is not executable by itself.


A phoxel can contribute to executable language structure only through promotion:


phoxel -> relation group -> structural candidate -> validated structure -> executable structure


This is the cleanest law.


10. What Core should optimize around


If this definition holds, Core should optimize for:


better phoxel formation

better phoxel relation quality

better world/image consistency

better integrity scoring

better promotion accuracy

lower illegal inference

graceful operation on current commodity cameras

future scalability without changing phoxel law


11. Hard summary sentence


A phoxel is the atomic dual-register observation object of Aurexis Core: a physically grounded, light-defined, image-accessed, world-anchored unit whose value comes primarily from lawful spatial relations, integrity-bounded interpretation, and promotion into larger validated visual structure.
